creator: stas@FreeBSD.org
text:
On Sun, 13 Apr 2008 15:12:28 -0500
"Jeremy Messenger" <mezz7@cox.net> mentioned:
 
> Jonathan, you will have to build libc, libpthread, boehm-gc and inkscape  
> with debug then reinstall those. When you get segfault, check in  
> inkscape's config.log and see where it crashes at, and try to use gdb to  
> collect the backtraces. I personal would upgrade to FreeBSD 7.x instead  
> all of that headaches.
> 

Jeremy, can we force inkscape to build and link a static version of boehm-gc
just like lang/Sather does? This way we'll allow people to have a customized
version of boehm-gc while inkscape will successfully work by building gc with
configuration it likes.

-- 
Stanislav Sedov
ST4096-RIPE
